steps.title: Understanding the system's constraint
steps.content: A point $(x, y)$ is a solution if it satisfies both inequalities simultaneously: $y > -3x - 12$ AND $y < -5x - 19$. For any valid solution, the $y$-value must lie strictly between the two linear expressions.

steps.title: Testing choice B
steps.content:
We need to find the table where all three points satisfy both inequalities.

Backsolving — testing choice B (points: $(-5,4)$, $(-6,10)$, $(-8,19)$):

For $(-5, 4)$:
  • $4 > -3(-5) - 12 = 15 - 12 = 3$? YES ✓
  • $4 < -5(-5) - 19 = 25 - 19 = 6$? YES ✓
For $(-6, 10)$:
  • $10 > -3(-6) - 12 = 18 - 12 = 6$? YES ✓
  • $10 < -5(-6) - 19 = 30 - 19 = 11$? YES ✓
For $(-8, 19)$:
  • $19 > -3(-8) - 12 = 24 - 12 = 12$? YES ✓
  • $19 < -5(-8) - 19 = 40 - 19 = 21$? YES ✓
All three points satisfy both conditions. That matches choice B — B is correct.
